title.en: A Single Patch Is Not Enough: Deterministic Fusion of Repair Candidates
title.zh: 单一补丁不足够：修复候选的确定性融合

abstract:
Modern LLM coding agents are commonly evaluated using pass@k, but developers typically apply a single final patch in real-world settings. This pass@k-to-pass@1 gap is a post-generation problem: a candidate patch pool may contain a correct patch, but the system must decide which one to suggest to developers. Existing post-generation approaches mainly rank whole candidates, filter them with tests, or query an LLM judge, but none deterministically reuse shared edit-atom evidence to both select and construct the final patch. Thus, we propose PatchFusion, a deterministic atomic evidence fusion approach for candidate patches that consults no test outcome at decision time. PatchFusion first fuses whole-diff agreement into a repair neighborhood, selects an auditable representative, and then applies evidence-constrained fusion (ECF) to retain repeated edit atoms and prune unsupported parts. To evaluate this setting, we build PatchFuseBench, a fixed-pool benchmark covering SWE-bench Verified, SWE-bench Multilingual, and Defects4J candidate patches.
  On PatchFuseBench, PatchFusion solves 426/500 bugs on SWE-bench Verified and 236/300 on SWE-bench Multilingual, and reaches 87/371 plausible patches on Defects4J, outperforming every matched candidate-pool selector on all three. PatchFusion recovers 41 and 27 bugs that no single source solves (30 and 18 more over the best single source). Ablation studies show that ECF adds +5/+6/+9 solved bugs by recovering in-pool repairs that selection misses, with no observed regression, and that PatchFusion's gains remain stable as candidate pools are resampled. On these complementary multi-source pools, cross-candidate evidence recovers more correct patches than the test-based and LLM-based selectors we evaluate, at orders-of-magnitude lower cost, reaching within 96.2% and 89.7% of the candidate-reachable ceiling on the two SWE-bench benchmarks.

relevance.en: This paper extends your work on patch validation and overfitting avoidance by proposing PatchFusion, a deterministic fusion of cross-candidate evidence that selects and constructs patches without test outcomes, outperforming test-based and LLM-based selectors on SWE-bench and Defects4J.
relevance.zh: 本文通过提出PatchFusion扩展了您在补丁验证和避免过拟合方面的工作，这是一种跨候选证据的确定性融合，可在没有测试结果的情况下选择和构建补丁，在SWE-bench和Defects4J上优于基于测试和基于LLM的选择器。

figures.label: Figure 1

caption.en: Motivating example (django__django-14315), where PatchFusion fuses repeated edit atoms into a resolved patch no candidate submitted.
caption.zh: 动机示例（django__django-14315），其中PatchFusion将重复的编辑原子融合成一个没有任何候选提交的已解决补丁。

figures.reference_text: II. MOTIVATING EXAMPLE Figure 1 shows a concrete case from SWE-bench Veri- fied, django__django-14315. The bug is in Django’s dbshell: when launching the database shell, the client passes env={} to subprocess.run, which replaces the child environment with an empty one instead of inherit- ing os.environ. The fix is to pass env or None so the child inherits the parent environment, and it spans 2 scopes: the PostgreSQL client must return env or None from settings_to_cmd_args_env, and the base client

figures.label: Figure 2

caption.en: PatchFusion turns a fixed candidate pool into one auditable final patch in four deterministic steps.
caption.zh: PatchFusion将固定候选池转化为一个可审计的最终补丁，通过四个确定性步骤。

figures.reference_text: PatchFusion turns a fixed candidate pool into one auditable final patch in four deterministic steps (Figure 2): (1) candi- date view and exact support collapses exact-duplicate diffs into support counts; (2) repair-neighborhood fusion groups candidates that touch related files and share edit vocabulary; (3) representative selection chooses one representative under the pool structure, admitting a local edit only under a same-surface guard; and (4) evidence-constrained fusion (ECF) keeps the edit atoms repeated across candidates and prunes unsupported ones.

figure.label: Figure 2

caption.en: PatchFusion turns a fixed candidate pool into one auditable final patch in four deterministic steps.
caption.zh: PatchFusion将固定候选池转化为一个可审计的最终补丁，通过四个确定性步骤。

figure.reference_text: PatchFusion turns a fixed candidate pool into one auditable final patch in four deterministic steps (Figure 2): (1) candi- date view and exact support collapses exact-duplicate diffs into support counts; (2) repair-neighborhood fusion groups candidates that touch related files and share edit vocabulary; (3) representative selection chooses one representative under the pool structure, admitting a local edit only under a same-surface guard; and (4) evidence-constrained fusion (ECF) keeps the edit atoms repeated across candidates and prunes unsupported ones.

brief_summary.en:
LLM-based program repair agents are typically evaluated by pass@k, but in practice only one patch can be deployed—creating a problematic pass@k-to-pass@1 gap. Existing selection methods rank whole candidates, filter by test results, or query an LLM judge, yet none exploit the repeated edit atoms that appear across multiple candidates. This misses a key opportunity both to identify when a correct patch exists and to construct one when no single candidate is fully correct. To address this, PatchFusion is proposed as a deterministic, evidence-based approach that fuses shared edit atoms from the candidate pool to select and construct the final patch without relying on test outcomes.

PatchFusion decomposes candidate diffs into edit atoms, aggregates support counts, then applies evidence-constrained fusion (ECF) to retain only atoms present in multiple candidates while pruning unsupported parts. On the PatchFuseBench benchmark, it solves 426/500 and 236/300 bugs on two SWE-bench benchmarks, outperforming every test-based and LLM-based selector at orders-of-magnitude lower cost (milliseconds per bug). It also recovers 41 and 27 bugs that no single candidate source solved, and ECF introduces no regression. These results demonstrate that deterministic fusion of cross-candidate evidence is a strong, cost-effective signal for patch correctness that avoids overfitting to test suites.
brief_summary.zh:
基于LLM的程序修复代理通常通过pass@k进行评估，但在实践中只能部署一个补丁，这造成了有问题的pass@k到pass@1的差距。现有的选择方法对整个候选进行排序、通过测试结果过滤或查询LLM评判器，但没有一种方法利用跨多个候选出现的重复编辑原子。这错过了一个关键机会，既无法识别何时存在正确的补丁，也无法在没有任何单个候选完全正确时构建一个。为了解决这个问题，提出了PatchFusion，这是一种确定性的、基于证据的方法，融合候选池中的共享编辑原子，以选择和构建最终补丁，而不依赖于测试结果。

PatchFusion将候选差异分解为编辑原子，聚合支持计数，然后应用证据约束融合（ECF）来仅保留出现在多个候选中的原子，同时修剪不支持的部分。在PatchFuseBench基准测试中，它在两个SWE-bench基准测试上解决了500个bug中的426个和300个bug中的236个，以数量级更低的成本（每bug毫秒级）优于每个基于测试和基于LLM的选择器。它还恢复了41个和27个没有任何单个候选源解决的bug，并且ECF没有引入回归。这些结果表明，跨候选证据的确定性融合是补丁正确性的一个强大且成本有效的信号，避免了过度拟合测试套件。

problem.en: The core problem is the pass@k-to-pass@1 gap in automated program repair with LLM coding agents. While agents are evaluated on pass@k (i.e., whether at least one candidate patch in a pool is correct), in practice developers must select a single final patch. Existing post-generation approaches rank whole candidates, filter with tests, or query an LLM judge, but none leverage shared edit-atom evidence across multiple candidates to both select and construct the final patch. This leaves a concrete gap: even when a correct patch exists in the pool, these methods often fail to identify it, and they cannot synthesize a correct patch that no single candidate contains. The problem matters because developers need a reliable, auditable, and cost-efficient way to obtain a single correct patch from a pool of candidates without relying on expensive or brittle test/LLM-based selection.
problem.zh: 核心问题是基于LLM编码代理的自动程序修复中的pass@k到pass@1的差距。虽然代理是通过pass@k评估的（即池中至少有一个候选补丁是正确的），但在实践中，开发者必须选择一个最终的补丁。现有的生成后方法对整个候选进行排序、用测试过滤或查询LLM评判器，但没有一种方法利用跨多个候选的共享编辑原子证据来选择和构建最终补丁。这留下了一个具体的差距：即使池中存在正确的补丁，这些方法通常也无法识别它，而且它们也无法合成任何单个候选都不包含的正确补丁。这个问题很重要，因为开发者需要一种可靠、可审计且经济高效的方式，从候选池中获得单个正确补丁，而不依赖昂贵或脆弱的基于测试/LLM的选择。

solution.en: PatchFusion is a deterministic atomic evidence fusion approach that selects and constructs a final patch from a pool of candidate patches without consulting test outcomes at decision time. Its key insight is that repeated edit atoms (e.g., line-level changes) across multiple independently generated candidates are strong evidence of correctness, and fusing such shared evidence can yield a correct patch even when no individual candidate is fully correct. Unlike prior work that ranks or filters whole diffs, PatchFusion decomposes candidates into edit atoms, aggregates support counts, and uses a novel evidence-constrained fusion (ECF) step to retain repeatedly occurring atoms while pruning unsupported parts. This makes the process auditable, deterministic (no randomness or model calls), and orders of magnitude cheaper than LLM-based selectors.
solution.zh: PatchFusion是一种确定性原子证据融合方法，它在候选补丁池中选择并构建最终补丁，而在决策时不参考测试结果。其关键见解是，多个独立生成的候选之间重复出现的编辑原子（例如，行级更改）是正确性的有力证据，融合这种共享证据可以产生正确的补丁，即使没有任何单个候选是完全正确的。与之前对完整差异进行排序或过滤的工作不同，PatchFusion将候选分解为编辑原子，聚合支持计数，并使用一种新颖的证据约束融合（ECF）步骤来保留重复出现的原子，同时修剪不支持的部分。这使得过程可审计、确定性（无随机性或模型调用），并且比基于LLM的选择器便宜多个数量级。

methodology.en: PatchFusion operates in four deterministic steps on a fixed candidate pool. Step 1 (Candidate View and Exact Support): Exact-duplicate diffs are collapsed into single entries with a support count reflecting how many candidates produced that exact diff. Step 2 (Repair-Neighborhood Fusion): Candidates that touch related files and share edit vocabulary are grouped into repair neighborhoods, capturing coherent patch families. Step 3 (Representative Selection): Within each neighborhood, a single representative diff is chosen based on the pool structure (e.g., highest support count) to provide an auditable base. Step 4 (Evidence-Constrained Fusion, ECF): The representative diff is refined by retaining only edit atoms that appear in at least k other candidates (k is a hyperparameter, set to 1 in the experiments) and discarding atoms that lack cross-candidate evidence. The output is a single fused patch. The entire pipeline requires no model or API calls; it runs in milliseconds. The methodology is illustrated in Figure 2: a diagram showing the four sequential steps with candidate pools flowing through view construction, neighborhood fusion, representative selection, and ECF to produce the final patch.
methodology.zh: PatchFusion在固定候选池上通过四个确定性步骤运行。步骤1（候选视图与精确支持）：完全相同的差异被折叠成单个条目，并附带一个支持计数，反映了有多少候选产生了该精确差异。步骤2（修复邻域融合）：涉及相关文件并共享编辑词汇的候选被分组到修复邻域中，捕获连贯的补丁族。步骤3（代表性选择）：在每个邻域内，基于池结构（例如，最高支持计数）选择一个代表性差异，以提供可审计的基础。步骤4（证据约束融合，ECF）：通过仅保留出现在至少k个其他候选中的编辑原子（k是一个超参数，在实验中设置为1）并丢弃缺乏跨候选证据的原子来精细化代表性差异。输出是一个单一的融合补丁。整个流程不需要模型或API调用；它在毫秒级内运行。该方法在图2中展示：一个图表显示了四个顺序步骤，候选池经过视图构建、邻域融合、代表性选择和ECF以产生最终补丁。

experiments.en: Experiments are conducted on PatchFuseBench, a fixed-pool benchmark covering SWE-bench Verified (500 bugs), SWE-bench Multilingual (300 bugs), and Defects4J (371 bugs). Candidate pools are generated from multiple LLM agents. Baselines include test-based selectors, LLM-based judges, and candidate-pool rankers. Metrics: number of bugs solved (pass@1) on SWE-bench and plausible patches on Defects4J. PatchFusion solves 426/500 on Verified (baselines: best single source 396, candidate-reachable ceiling 443), 236/300 on Multilingual (best single source 218, ceiling 263), and achieves 87/371 plausible on Defects4J. It recovers 41 and 27 bugs that no single source solves (30 and 18 more than the best single source). Ablation: removing ECF reduces solves by +5/+6/+9 bugs with zero regression. Cost: 3.28 ms per bug (median over 7 runs, no model calls), placing it in the top-left corner of the cost-vs-repairs plot (Figure 3). It reaches within 96.2% and 89.7% of the candidate-reachable ceiling on the two SWE-bench benchmarks.
experiments.zh: 实验在PatchFuseBench上进行，这是一个固定池基准，覆盖SWE-bench Verified（500个bug）、SWE-bench Multilingual（300个bug）和Defects4J（371个bug）。候选池由多个LLM代理生成。基线包括基于测试的选择器、基于LLM的评判器和候选池排序器。指标：SWE-bench上解决的bug数量（pass@1）和Defects4J上的合理补丁数量。PatchFusion在Verified上解决了426/500（基线：最佳单一来源396，候选可达上限443），在Multilingual上解决了236/300（最佳单一来源218，上限263），并在Defects4J上达到了87/371的合理补丁。它恢复了41个和27个没有任何单一来源解决的bug（分别比最佳单一来源多30个和18个）。消融：移除ECF会减少+5/+6/+9个已解决bug，且零回归。成本：每bug 3.28毫秒（7次运行的中位数，无模型调用），使其位于成本-修复图（图3）的左上角。它在两个SWE-bench基准上达到了候选可达上限的96.2%和89.7%。

conclusion.en: PatchFusion shows that deterministically fusing repeated edit atoms across repair candidates can reliably construct correct patches, outperforming test-based and LLM-based selectors at much lower cost while avoiding overfitting. The creation of PatchFuseBench provides a standardized fixed-pool evaluation for patch selection, complementing existing benchmarks. These findings mean that shared evidence across candidate patches is a strong signal for patch correctness, and that lightweight deterministic fusion can be effectively integrated into LLM-based repair agents as a post-generation selection step. However, the approach depends on having a diverse and sufficient candidate pool, as it cannot invent repairs absent from the candidates.
conclusion.zh: PatchFusion表明，确定性地融合跨修复候选的重复编辑原子可以可靠地构建正确的补丁，以更低的成本优于基于测试和基于LLM的选择器，同时避免过拟合。PatchFuseBench的创建为补丁选择提供了标准化的固定池评估，补充了现有基准。这些发现意味着候选补丁之间的共享证据是补丁正确性的强有力信号，并且轻量级的确定性融合可以有效地集成到基于LLM的修复代理中，作为生成后的选择步骤。然而，该方法依赖于多样且充足的候选池，因为它无法发明候选池中不存在的修复。

question.en: How does PatchFusion's evidence-constrained fusion method compare with test-based patch validation in terms of reducing overfitting (plausible but incorrect patches)?
question.zh: PatchFusion的证据约束融合方法与基于测试的补丁验证在减少过拟合（看似正确但不正确的补丁）方面相比如何？

reason.en: Directly addresses the user's interest in avoiding overfitting patches and patch validation. The paper discusses how ECF retains repeated edit atoms and prunes unsupported parts, which may reduce plausible but incorrect patches that might pass tests. This question probes the effectiveness of deterministic fusion as an alternative to test-based validation.
reason.zh: 直接回应用户对避免过拟合补丁和补丁验证的兴趣。论文讨论了ECF如何保留重复的编辑原子并修剪不支持的部分，这可能会减少可能通过测试但看似正确却不正确的补丁。这个问题探讨了确定性融合作为基于测试验证的替代方案的有效性。

question.en: What are the strengths and limitations of using PatchFuseBench as a benchmark for evaluating patch selection methods, and how does it complement existing benchmarks like Defects4J and SWE-bench?
question.zh: 使用PatchFuseBench作为评估补丁选择方法的基准有哪些优势和局限性？它如何补充现有的基准如Defects4J和SWE-bench？

reason.en: Aligns with the user's interest in benchmark releases and empirical studies. PatchFuseBench is a new fixed-pool benchmark built from existing ones. Understanding its design and role helps assess its value for future APR research.
reason.zh: 与用户对基准发布和实证研究的兴趣一致。PatchFuseBench是一个从现有基准构建的新固定池基准。理解其设计和作用有助于评估其对未来APR研究的价值。

question.en: To what extent do the cross-candidate evidence fusion results suggest that deterministic methods can outperform test-outcome-based or LLM-based selection, and what are the implications for integrating such methods into LLM-driven repair agents?
question.zh: 跨候选证据融合的结果在多大程度上表明确定性方法可以优于基于测试结果或基于LLM的选择？将这些方法集成到LLM驱动的修复代理中有什么意义？

reason.en: Connects to the user's interest in LLM-driven agents and combination of techniques. The paper shows PatchFusion outperforms test-based and LLM-based selectors at lower cost. This question explores how deterministic fusion could be integrated into LLM-based APR systems.
reason.zh: 与用户对LLM驱动代理和技术组合的兴趣相关。论文显示PatchFusion以更低成本优于基于测试和基于LLM的选择器。这个问题探讨了如何将确定性融合集成到基于LLM的APR系统中。

question.en: How does PatchFusion's repair-neighborhood fusion step relate to fault localization, and could this approach be extended to incorporate additional static or dynamic analysis information?
question.zh: PatchFusion的修复邻域融合步骤与故障定位有何关系？这种方法能否扩展以纳入额外的静态或动态分析信息？

reason.en: Touches on fault localization and cross-cutting connections with static/dynamic analysis. The repair-neighborhood fusion identifies shared edit regions. This question probes whether the method could be enhanced by incorporating more precise localization information.
reason.zh: 涉及故障定位以及与静态/动态分析的交叉联系。修复邻域融合识别共享编辑区域。这个问题探讨了是否可以通过纳入更精确的定位信息来增强该方法。

question.en: How does PatchFusion's evidence-constrained fusion method compare with test-based patch validation in terms of reducing overfitting (plausible but incorrect patches)?
question.zh: PatchFusion的证据约束融合方法与基于测试的补丁验证在减少过拟合（看似正确但不正确的补丁）方面相比如何？

reason.en: Directly addresses the user's interest in avoiding overfitting patches and patch validation. The paper discusses how ECF retains repeated edit atoms and prunes unsupported parts, which may reduce plausible but incorrect patches that might pass tests. This question probes the effectiveness of deterministic fusion as an alternative to test-based validation.
reason.zh: 直接回应用户对避免过拟合补丁和补丁验证的兴趣。论文讨论了ECF如何保留重复的编辑原子并修剪不支持的部分，这可能会减少可能通过测试但看似正确却不正确的补丁。这个问题探讨了确定性融合作为基于测试验证的替代方案的有效性。

answer.en: PatchFusion's evidence-constrained fusion (ECF) method reduces overfitting by using deterministic atomic evidence from repeated edit atoms across candidates to prune unsupported parts, rather than relying on test outcomes that may select plausible but incorrect patches. The paper highlights that PatchFusion "consults no test outcome at decision time" and shows that "cross-candidate evidence recovers more correct patches than the test-based and LLM-based selectors we evaluate." ECF's ability to retain repeated edit atoms and prune unsupported parts without observed regression ("ablation studies show that ECF adds +5/+6/+9 solved bugs... with no observed regression") contrasts with test-based validation, which can be vulnerable to overfitting to test suites, whereas PatchFusion's deterministic approach avoids such pitfalls.
answer.zh: PatchFusion的证据约束融合（ECF）方法通过利用来自候选间重复编辑原子的确定性原子证据来修剪不支持的部分，而不是依赖可能选择看似正确但实际不正确的补丁的测试结果，从而减少过拟合。论文强调PatchFusion“在决策时不参考任何测试结果”，并表明“跨候选证据比我们评估的基于测试和基于LLM的选择器恢复了更多正确的补丁。”ECF能够保留重复的编辑原子并修剪不支持的部分，且未观察到回归（“消融研究表明ECF额外解决了+5/+6/+9个bug……且未观察到任何回归”），这与基于测试的验证形成对比，后者可能容易过度拟合测试套件，而PatchFusion的确定性方法避免了这些陷阱。

question.en: What are the strengths and limitations of using PatchFuseBench as a benchmark for evaluating patch selection methods, and how does it complement existing benchmarks like Defects4J and SWE-bench?
question.zh: 使用PatchFuseBench作为评估补丁选择方法的基准有哪些优势和局限性？它如何补充现有的基准如Defects4J和SWE-bench？

reason.en: Aligns with the user's interest in benchmark releases and empirical studies. PatchFuseBench is a new fixed-pool benchmark built from existing ones. Understanding its design and role helps assess its value for future APR research.
reason.zh: 与用户对基准发布和实证研究的兴趣一致。PatchFuseBench是一个从现有基准构建的新固定池基准。理解其设计和作用有助于评估其对未来APR研究的价值。

answer.en: PatchFuseBench is a fixed-pool benchmark built from candidate patches of SWE-bench Verified, SWE-bench Multilingual, and Defects4J, as stated: 'a fixed-pool benchmark covering SWE-bench Verified, SWE-bench Multilingual, and Defects4J candidate patches.' Its strength lies in providing a standardized, deterministic evaluation for patch selection methods, enabling fair comparison across existing benchmarks. However, the paper does not explicitly discuss its limitations; therefore, we cannot identify specific weaknesses from the provided text. It complements Defects4J and SWE-bench by offering a curated pool of candidate patches specifically for evaluating the post-generation selection step, which these benchmarks originally do not cover.
answer.zh: PatchFuseBench是一个固定池基准，由SWE-bench Verified、SWE-bench Multilingual和Defects4J的候选补丁构建而成，如所述：“一个覆盖SWE-bench Verified、SWE-bench Multilingual和Defects4J候选补丁的固定池基准。”其优势在于为补丁选择方法提供标准化的确定性评估，使得跨现有基准的公平比较成为可能。然而，论文并未明确讨论其局限性；因此，我们无法从提供的文本中识别具体弱点。它通过提供一个专门用于评估生成后选择步骤的候选补丁池来补充Defects4J和SWE-bench，而这些基准最初并未覆盖这一步骤。

question.en: To what extent do the cross-candidate evidence fusion results suggest that deterministic methods can outperform test-outcome-based or LLM-based selection, and what are the implications for integrating such methods into LLM-driven repair agents?
question.zh: 跨候选证据融合的结果在多大程度上表明确定性方法可以优于基于测试结果或基于LLM的选择？将这些方法集成到LLM驱动的修复代理中有什么意义？

reason.en: Connects to the user's interest in LLM-driven agents and combination of techniques. The paper shows PatchFusion outperforms test-based and LLM-based selectors at lower cost. This question explores how deterministic fusion could be integrated into LLM-based APR systems.
reason.zh: 与用户对LLM驱动代理和技术组合的兴趣相关。论文显示PatchFusion以更低成本优于基于测试和基于LLM的选择器。这个问题探讨了如何将确定性融合集成到基于LLM的APR系统中。

answer.en: The paper's results strongly suggest that deterministic methods can outperform test-outcome-based and LLM-based selection, as PatchFusion 'outperforms every matched candidate-pool selector on all three' benchmarks and 'cross-candidate evidence recovers more correct patches than the test-based and LLM-based selectors we evaluate, at orders-of-magnitude lower cost.' Specifically, PatchFusion solves 426/500 and 236/300 bugs on two SWE-bench benchmarks. These findings imply that such deterministic fusion can be integrated into LLM-driven repair agents as a lightweight, cost-effective post-generation stage to select and construct final patches from candidate pools, reducing reliance on expensive test suites or LLM judges while achieving near-optimal performance (within 96.2% and 89.7% of the reachable ceiling).
answer.zh: 论文的结果强烈表明，确定性方法可以优于基于测试结果和基于LLM的选择，因为PatchFusion“在所有三个基准上优于每个匹配的候选池选择器”，并且“跨候选证据比我们评估的基于测试和基于LLM的选择器恢复了更多正确的补丁，成本降低了多个数量级。”具体来说，PatchFusion在两个SWE-bench基准上分别解决了426/500和236/300个bug。这些发现意味着，这种确定性融合可以作为轻量级、成本有效的生成后阶段集成到LLM驱动的修复代理中，从候选池中选择和构建最终补丁，减少对昂贵的测试套件或LLM评判器的依赖，同时实现接近最优的性能（达到可达上限的96.2%和89.7%）。

question.en: How does PatchFusion's repair-neighborhood fusion step relate to fault localization, and could this approach be extended to incorporate additional static or dynamic analysis information?
question.zh: PatchFusion的修复邻域融合步骤与故障定位有何关系？这种方法能否扩展以纳入额外的静态或动态分析信息？

reason.en: Touches on fault localization and cross-cutting connections with static/dynamic analysis. The repair-neighborhood fusion identifies shared edit regions. This question probes whether the method could be enhanced by incorporating more precise localization information.
reason.zh: 涉及故障定位以及与静态/动态分析的交叉联系。修复邻域融合识别共享编辑区域。这个问题探讨了是否可以通过纳入更精确的定位信息来增强该方法。

answer.en: The paper does not explicitly relate the repair-neighborhood fusion step to fault localization; it describes fusing "whole-diff agreement into a repair neighborhood" purely from candidate patch diffs, without consulting tests or fault localization. The approach relies on "cross-candidate evidence" and does not incorporate static or dynamic analysis—it is entirely based on edit-atom repetition across patches. Thus, extension to include additional analysis is not addressed and would require new mechanisms.
answer.zh: 论文未明确将修复邻域融合步骤与故障定位联系起来；它描述了纯粹从候选补丁差异中“将整个差异的一致性融合到修复邻域”，而未参考测试或故障定位。该方法依赖于“跨候选证据”，并不包含静态或动态分析——它完全基于跨补丁的编辑原子重复。因此，扩展到包含额外分析的问题未得到解决，需要新的机制。
